tagline: Modified bitumen and built-up roofing for low-slope residential and commercial.
metaDescription: Flat roof installation and replacement in Los Angeles County — modified bitumen, built-up, and single-ply systems. Free inspections. CA Lic. #1109337.
intro_h2: Flat & Low-Slope Roofing in LA County
intro: Flat roofs are common on mid-century homes, additions, garages, and virtually all commercial buildings in LA County. Palm Crest Roofing installs modified bitumen (mod-bit), built-up roofing (BUR), and single-ply systems (TPO, PVC) for low-slope applications. We also handle hybrid roofs with tile or shingle sections.
benefits: Built-up roofing (BUR) with gravel or cap sheet
Single-ply membranes (TPO, PVC) for commercial and large residential
Proper slope and drainage design to prevent ponding
Primer, base sheet, cap sheet, and proper flashing at all penetrations

process.title: Inspection & Scope
process.desc: Assess existing roof, drains, penetrations, and slope. Recommend the right system for your building.

process.title: Tear-Off
process.desc: Full tear-off to the deck when needed, or clean prep for a recover when appropriate.

process.title: Deck Repair & Insulation
process.desc: Replace rotted deck, install or upgrade insulation and cover board for energy efficiency.

process.title: Base & Cap
process.desc: Base sheet and cap sheet (or membrane) installed with mechanical fasteners, adhesive, or torch per system.

process.title: Flashings & Drains
process.desc: New flashings at parapets, walls, drains, and penetrations. Ensure all drainage paths are clear.

faqs.q: How long does a flat roof last?
faqs.a: Modified bitumen: 15–20 years. Built-up with cap sheet: 20–25 years. TPO single-ply: 15–25 years. Proper drainage and ventilation extend life significantly.

faqs.q: Why does my flat roof keep leaking?
faqs.a: Most flat-roof leaks are at penetrations, drains, or walls — not the field of the roof. Often a targeted flashing repair fixes it. We'll diagnose honestly during your free inspection.

faqs.q: Can you install a cool roof?
faqs.a: Yes. Reflective cap sheets and white TPO/PVC membranes meet Title 24 cool-roof requirements and reduce cooling costs.
